segments.text: One Sabbath Jesus was passing through the grainfields, and His disciples began to pick the heads of grain, rub them in their hands, and eat them.
But some of the Pharisees asked,
Why are you doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?
segments.speaker: some of the Pharisees

segments.text: Jesus replied,
Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He entered the house of God, took the consecrated bread and gave it to his companions, and ate what is lawful only for the priests to eat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus declared,
The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: On another Sabbath Jesus entered the synagogue and was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was withered.
Looking for a reason to accuse Jesus, the scribes and Pharisees were watching Him closely to see if He would heal on the Sabbath.
But Jesus knew their thoughts and said to the man with the withered hand,
Get up and st and among us.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he got up and stood there.
Then Jesus said to them,
I ask you, which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And after looking around at all of them, He said to the man,
Stretch out your hand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He did so, and it was restored.
But the scribes and Pharisees were filled with rage and began to discuss with one another what they might do to Jesus.
In those days Jesus went out to the mountain to pray, and He spent the night in prayer to God.
When daylight came, He called His disciples to Him and chose twelve of them, whom He also designated as apostles:
Simon, whom He named Peter, and his brother Andrew; James and John; Philip and Bartholomew;
Matthew and Thomas; James son of Alphaeus and Simon called the Zealot;
Judas son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who became a traitor.

Then Jesus came down with them and stood on a level place. A large crowd of His disciples was there, along with a great number of people from all over Judea, Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon.
They had come to hear Him and to be healed of their diseases, and those troubled by unclean spirits were healed.
The entire crowd was trying to touch Him, because power was coming from Him and healing them all.
Looking up at His disciples, Jesus said:
Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you will be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you will laugh.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blessed are you when people hate you, and when they exclude you and insult you and reject your name as evil because of the Son of Man.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, because great is your reward in heaven. For their fathers treated the prophets in the same way.

## Woes to the Satisfied
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But woe to you who are rich, for you have already received your comfort.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you who are well fed now, for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you will mourn and weep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Woe to you when all men speak well of you, for their fathers treated the false prophets in the same way.

## Love Your Enemies
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But to those of you who will listen, I say: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: bless those who curse you, pray for those who mistreat you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If someone strikes you on one cheek, turn to him the other also. And if someone takes your cloak, do not withhold your tunic as well.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what is yours, do not demand it back.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do to others as you would have them do to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners love those who love them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? Even sinners do the same.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And if you lend to those from whom you expect repayment, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, expecting to be repaid in full.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But love your enemies, do good to them, and lend to them, expecting nothing in return. Then your reward will be great, and you will be sons of the Most High; for He is kind to the ungrateful and wicked.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.

## Judging Others
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive, and you will be forgiven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured back to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus also told them a parable:
Can a blind man lead a blind man? Will they not both fall into a pit?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone who is fully trained will be like his teacher.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do you look at the speck in your brother’s eye, but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
How can you say, ‘Brother, let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while you yourself fail to see the beam in your own eye? You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.

## A Tree and Its Fruit
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No good tree bears bad fruit, nor does a bad tree bear good fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For each tree is known by its own fruit. Indeed, figs are not gathered from thornbushes, nor grapes from brambles.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
The good man brings good things out of the good treasure of his heart, and the evil man brings evil things out of the evil treasure of his heart. For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.

## The House on the Rock
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do you call Me ‘Lord, Lord,’ but not do what I say?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I will show you what he is like who comes to Me and hears My words and acts on them:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug down deep and laid his foundation on the rock. When the flood came, the torrent crashed against that house but could not shake it, because it was well built.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the one who hears My words and does not act on them is like a man who built his house on ground without a foundation. The torrent crashed against that house, and immediately it fell—and great was its destruction!
segments.speaker: Jesus
